tools.name: list_currencies
tools.description: List all coins GhostSwap supports for swapping (1,600+ assets). Use lite=true for just an array of tickers (e.g. ["btc","eth","ltc"]).

tools.name: get_pair
tools.description: Get the min and max swap amount for a single (from, to) pair. Returns separate min/max for float-rate and fixed-rate modes.

tools.name: validate_address
tools.description: Check whether a wallet address is valid for a given currency. Returns {valid:true} or {valid:false,message}. Permissive pre-check — create_swap runs a stricter validator.

tools.name: get_quote
tools.description: Get a live swap quote — rate, amountUserReceives, network fee, min/max. Always display amountUserReceives (not amountTo). mode="fixed" locks the rate and returns a short-lived rateId.

tools.name: create_swap
tools.description: Create a new swap. Returns payinAddress where the end-user must send funds. REQUIRES refundAddress (the user's own wallet on the FROM chain). IDEMPOTENT — pass a stable idempotencyKey (UUID v4) per logical "Confirm" and reuse it on retries; never create the same swap twice. If a result has stop:true

tools.name: get_swap
tools.description: Get the current status of a swap by id. Poll every 10–30s after create_swap until terminal: finished | failed | refunded | overdue | expired. `hold` = AML/KYC review (slow polling; direct end-user to support@ghostswap.io).

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: validate_address, get_swap · Private data: validate_address, create_swap · Public sink: create_swap.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: validate_address, get_swap · Private data: validate_address, create_swap · Public sink: create_swap

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
